title: Product Activation & Time-to-Value
business_definition: Activation is the moment a new user experiences the core value of the product for the first time. Activation rate = users who hit the activation event / total signups. Low activation is the leading cause of early churn.
diagnostic_questions: What is the activation event — has its definition changed recently?
How long does it take users to activate — is median time-to-activation increasing?
Which step in onboarding has the highest drop-off?
Do activated users retain significantly better than non-activated users?
Is low activation a volume problem (fewer users attempting) or a conversion problem (more attempts failing)?

causal_relationships.detection_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('week', signup_date) AS week, COUNT(*) AS signups, COUNT(CASE WHEN activated_at IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS activated, COUNT(CASE WHEN activated_at IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS activation_rate FROM users GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('week', signup_date) AS week, AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (activated_at - signup_date)) / 3600) AS avg_hours_to_activation FROM users WHERE activated_at IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

inflation_causes.cause: Activation event set too low — users 'activate' without finding real value
inflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT activated, AVG(day_30_retained) AS retention_at_30d FROM (SELECT u.user_id, u.activated_at IS NOT NULL AS activated, MAX(CASE WHEN activity_date <= signup_date + INTERVAL '30 days' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS day_30_retained FROM users u LEFT JOIN user_activity a USING (user_id) GROUP BY 1, 2) t GROUP BY 1

deflation_causes.cause: Activation event requires a backend job that is delayed — false low count
deflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE signup_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '2 days' AND activated_at IS NULL

anti_patterns: Do not use page views or logins as the activation event — they have no correlation with retention
Do not aggregate activation rate across very different user cohorts (e.g. enterprise vs SMB trials)

sql_assets.activation_funnel: SELECT step_name, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS users, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) * 100.0 / FIRST_VALUE(COUNT(DISTINCT user_id)) OVER (ORDER BY step_order) AS pct_of_top FROM onboarding_events GROUP BY step_name, step_order ORDER BY step_order
